problem_statement:
fix: make "file://" fail `url` validation

"file://" is not a valid URL since it has no path.

## Fixes Or Enhances

Makes `"file://"` fail the `url` validation instead of passing it.

This is akin to `"http://"` already failing as expected in tests. 

**Make sure that you've checked the boxes below before you submit PR:**
- [x] Tests exist or have been written that cover this particular change.

@go-playground/validator-maintainers
test_patch:
diff --git a/validator_test.go b/validator_test.go
index 1273b47..2fcb3ff 100644
--- a/validator_test.go
+++ b/validator_test.go
@@ -8255,7 +8255,9 @@ func TestUrl(t *testing.T) {
 		{"file:///c:/Windows/file.txt", true},
 		{"file://localhost/path/to/file.txt", true},
 		{"file://localhost/c:/WINDOWS/file.txt", true},
-		{"file://", true},
+		{"file:", false},
+		{"file:/", false},
+		{"file://", false},
 		{"file:////remotehost/path/file.txt", true},
 	}
